routes.title: Routes
routes.helpIntro: Here you only view and optimize routes for bookings that already exist. Create and edit bookings in Schedule or Bookings. For visits at the client’s address, use off-site execution and fill in the address so we can geocode it.
routes.mapHelpHint: Drag to move the map. Each visit has its own color on the line and markers; amber = return leg when shown. S/E = same home for start and return.
routes.mapNoGeoHint: Nothing to show on the map yet. Add a service address for off-site visits so stops can appear here.
routes.mapNoTokenHint: The map cannot load because the map service is not configured. Stops are still listed below. Ask an administrator to set the public map token in the deployment environment.
routes.addHomeAddress: Add a home address for this specialist
routes.addHomeAddressHint: Routes start and end from this location. You can add it in Team settings.
routes.helpCompact: Tip: create and edit visits in
routes.optimizeSingleVisitHint: Only one client visit on this route: stop order cannot change. Use Recalculate to refresh driving distance and the line on the map. Add more visits to let optimization reorder stops.
routes.previewSuppressedWorse: The suggested order wouldn't shorten your route on the road. Your current order is kept.
routes.addressNotOnMapYet: Address is not on the map yet — check the booking after saving.
routes.stopOffsiteAddressMissing: No visit address for {client}. Add an address on the booking (off-site) or in the client profile.

savedRoutes.title: Saved routes by day
savedRoutes.hint: Click a date to open that day’s route.
savedRoutes.empty: No saved routes for this specialist yet.

preview.title: Optimization preview
preview.insufficientData: Not enough data to compare routes. Set specialist home or company location.
preview.summaryImproved: Road distance is shorter with the proposed order (HERE estimate).
preview.summaryWorse: Road distance is longer with the proposed order (HERE estimate). You can still apply it or cancel.
preview.summaryTie: Stop order would change, but road distance is about the same (HERE estimate).

stats.periodGridNoteMonth: Only bookings in the current calendar month (from the 1st through the last day). Amounts use each slot’s total.

discounts.title: Discounts & promo codes
discounts.description: Configure loyalty tiers (by completed bookings) and promo codes for clients.

rule.hint: Which bookings count toward loyalty tiers

tiers.emptyHint: Add tiers to reward repeat bookings with automatic discounts.
tiers.sequenceHint: Add tiers in ascending order of the threshold (smaller first, then larger). The customer gets one tier — the highest threshold they have reached. The “upper cap” field is rarely needed; leave it empty in most cases.
tiers.name: Name
tiers.minBookingsHelp: Minimum counted bookings for this tier to apply. Each next tier should use a higher number.
tiers.advancedCap: Optional: cap the range at the top
tiers.maxBookingsHelp: Usually leave empty: the tier stays in effect for any count above the threshold until a higher tier applies. Use only if you need a hard ceiling for this tier.

promos.emptyHint: Create a code clients can use when they book with you.
promos.name: Title

routes.title: Routes are a Pro feature
routes.description: Routes are available on the Professional and Enterprise plans. Upgrade to optimize daily trips for your specialists.

analytics.title: Reports are a Pro feature
analytics.description: Detailed reports and analytics are available on the Professional and Enterprise plans. Upgrade to track bookings, revenue and team performance in depth.

api_access.title: API access is an Enterprise feature
api_access.description: Read-only API tokens, exports to CRM/BI, and integrations are included on the Enterprise plan.

api.title: Developer API
api.description: Create tokens and call the read-only API for your company data (services, clients, bookings).

tokens.emptyHint: Create a token to connect CRMs, scripts, or internal tools.
tokens.name: Token name

keyDialog.title: Token identifier
keyDialog.intro: For security, the full secret is only shown once when you create the token. The saved prefix below lets you recognize this token in logs or support; create a new token if you need a fresh secret.
keyDialog.noPrefix: No prefix is stored for this token.

revokeDialog.title: Revoke token
revokeDialog.message: Revoke “{name}”? Integrations using this token will stop working.

createdToken.title: Copy your token now
createdToken.warning: You will not be able to see this token again. Store it in a password manager or secret store.

docs.intro: Authenticate with a Bearer token. All endpoints are read-only (GET) and scoped to your company.
docs.endpointSchedule: List schedule slots from confirmed bookings
docs.pagination: Pagination: query params page and per_page (max 100, default 25).
docs.filtersBookings: Bookings: optional status, from, to (or date_from, date_to), service_id, specialist_id.
docs.filtersClients: Clients: optional search, status, created_from, created_to.
docs.filtersTeamMembers: Team members: optional include_inactive (default: active only).
docs.filtersReviews: Reviews: optional service_id, rating, created_from, created_to.
docs.filtersSchedule: Schedule: same date/status/service/specialist filters as bookings list.
docs.rateLimits: Rate limits: 60 requests per minute per token and 5000 per day per company (unless overridden on the plan).
docs.errorExample: JSON body includes error and message fields; HTTP 401/403/404/429 as usual.
docs.authHint: Create a token on the Tokens tab and pass it in this header.
docs.exampleHint: Copy the command below and replace YOUR_TOKEN with a token from the Tokens tab.
docs.exampleTokenHint: Uses the production host so the command works as-is after you paste your token.
docs.baseUrlIntro: Send requests to API_ROOT/v1. API_ROOT is your Laravel API base URL (it normally ends with /api). Example: if API_ROOT is https://api.rexten.live/api, the v1 prefix is https://api.rexten.live/api/v1.
docs.baseUrlPatternHint: Replace BASE_URL with your API root including /api (no trailing slash before /v1).
docs.baseUrlThisAppHint: When you use localhost or staging, this value comes from NEXT_PUBLIC_LARAVEL_API_URL or the app’s proxy rules.
docs.responseListIntro: List endpoints return an array under data and pagination under meta (page, per_page, total, last_page).
docs.responseSingleIntro: GET …/{id} returns one object under data; meta is omitted.
docs.responseMeNote: GET /v1/me returns a nested object under data (company and subscription usage), without meta.

subscription.title: Subscription
subscription.description: Choose a plan that fits your business needs

trial.daysLeft: {days, plural, one {# day left} other {# days left}}
trial.bannerHint: Add a payment method before the trial ends to keep all features. Otherwise, your workspace switches to the Free plan.

subscription.stripeDisabled: Online payments are currently disabled. Contact support.
subscription.cancelSuccess: Subscription canceled now. Paid features stay active until {endDate}.
subscription.cancelSuccessNoDate: Subscription canceled now. Paid features stay active until the end of the billing period.

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: Perfect for getting started with basic tools

professional.name: Professional
professional.description: For growing businesses that need more power

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For large teams with advanced requirements

cancelDialog.title: Cancel subscription?
cancelDialog.message: You keep full paid access until {endDate}. After that, your workspace moves to the free plan and you get a 7-day grace period to bring team members, services and ads within the free-plan limits — anything above the limit after the grace period will be deactivated automatically.
cancelDialog.messageNoEnd: You keep full paid access until the end of the billing period. After that, your workspace moves to the free plan and you get a 7-day grace period to bring team members, services and ads within the free-plan limits — anything above the limit after the grace period will be deactivated automatically.

subscription.resumeSuccess: Subscription resumed. Cancellation has been withdrawn.

successDialog.title: Subscription activated!
successDialog.message: Your plan has been activated. Enjoy all the features!

item1.title: Mobile app for clients
item1.desc: iOS and Android apps for your customers to book services

item2.title: Advanced reporting
item2.desc: Detailed analytics, custom reports, and data export

item3.title: Integrations
item3.desc: Connect with Google Calendar, Zapier, and more

item4.title: Multi-location support
item4.desc: Manage multiple business locations from one account

faq.q1: Can I change my plan later?
faq.a1: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. Changes take effect immediately.
faq.q2: What payment methods do you accept?
faq.a2: We accept all major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express) via Stripe.
faq.a3: We offer a 14-day free trial for all new accounts. No credit card required to start.
faq.a4: You can cancel anytime. Your subscription remains active until the end of the billing period.

limitReached.title: Plan limit reached
limitReached.description: Upgrade your subscription to add more.
limitReached.teamMembers: You have reached the limit for team members and staff seats.
limitReached.services: You have reached the limit for services.
limitReached.advertisements: You have reached the limit for listings and advertisements.
limitReached.scheduledDowngrade: {resource} You have a scheduled plan downgrade — please reduce the excess before it takes effect or it will be deactivated automatically.
limitReached.scheduledDowngradeWithDate: {resource} You have a scheduled plan downgrade on {endDate} — please reduce the excess before that date or it will be deactivated automatically after the grace period.

downgrade.dialogIntro: You keep your current plan and limits until the billing period ends. After that, the lower plan applies. If you exceed the new limits, deactivate resources on this page — nothing is deleted automatically.
downgrade.scheduledSuccess: Downgrade scheduled for the end of the billing period.

upgradeDialog.title: Confirm upgrade?
upgradeDialog.intro: Your plan will switch to the higher tier immediately. Stripe charges a prorated amount right away to the card on file for this subscription (the exact amount depends on how much time is left in your current billing period).
upgradeDialog.note: If the charge is declined, your plan will not change and you may be redirected to Stripe Checkout to complete payment with another card.
upgradeDialog.successNote: The prorated charge was applied to your saved payment method. Check your email for the receipt.

overLimit.bannerBody: Deactivate extra resources or upgrade your plan. Nothing is removed automatically.

resolveLimits.title: Bring usage within plan limits
resolveLimits.description: Deactivate items you do not need to keep active. Data is kept; you can reactivate later where the product allows.

featureRequired.title: Upgrade required
featureRequired.description: This feature is not included in your current plan.
featureRequired.analytics: Advanced reports and analytics are available on higher plans.

serverMessages.stripe_disabled: Online payments are currently disabled. Contact support.

serverMessages.period_ended: The billing period has already ended.
serverMessages.use_cancel_for_free: To switch to the free plan, cancel your paid subscription first.

serverMessages.already_subscribed: You already have an active paid subscription. Cancel it first to change plans.

onboardingTour.welcomeSubtitle: Manage your clients, bookings, and services in one place.
onboardingTour.welcomeBody: This quick tour will show you how to use the admin panel.
onboardingTour.welcomePreviewNavText: Switch sections from the sidebar: dashboard, schedule, bookings, routes, and more—no hunting through tabs.
onboardingTour.welcomePreviewScheduleText: Pick a slot, see who’s booked, and open a booking—your day flows in one view.
onboardingTour.welcomePreviewClientsText: CRM keeps contacts, notes, and history together so you always know who you’re serving.
onboardingTour.restartHint: Replay the guided tour any time.

sidebar.title: Navigation
sidebar.text: All sections of your business admin are here. We will walk through the main items in the menu.

section1.title: 1. What are Cookies
section1.content: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile phone) when you visit a website. Cookies help the platform remember your preferences and improve your user experience.

subsection1.title: 2.1. Essential Cookies
subsection1.content: These cookies are necessary for the platform to function and cannot be switched off. They are usually set in response to actions made by you, such as logging in or filling in forms.

list.item1: Session cookies to maintain your login session
list.item2: Security cookies to protect against fraud
list.item3: Preference cookies to save your settings

subsection2.title: 2.2. Functional Cookies
subsection2.content: These cookies allow the platform to remember your choices and provide enhanced features:

subsection3.title: 2.3. Analytical Cookies
subsection3.content: These cookies help us understand how visitors use the platform, allowing us to improve functionality and user experience:

section3.title: 3. Purposes of Cookie Usage
section3.content: We use cookies for the following purposes:

list.item4: Analyzing platform usage to improve service
list.item5: Ensuring correct operation of all features

section4.title: 4. Third-Party Cookies
section4.content: Some cookies may be set by third-party services we use on the platform. These may include:
section4.note: These third-party services have their own privacy and cookie policies. We recommend reviewing their policies.

subsection1.title: 5.1. Browser Settings
subsection1.content: Most browsers allow you to manage cookies through settings. You can:

list.item4: Receive notifications before cookies are set

subsection1.note: Please note that disabling cookies may affect platform functionality and your user experience.

subsection2.title: 5.2. Platform Settings
subsection2.content: You can manage some cookies through your account settings on the platform. Access to settings is available in the profile section.

list.item1: Session Cookies — deleted when you close your browser
list.item2: Persistent Cookies — remain on your device for a specific period or until manually deleted

section7.title: 7. Compliance with US Law
section7.content: The use of cookies on the platform complies with US legal requirements, including Federal Trade Commission (FTC) requirements and specific California state requirements (CCPA), where applicable.
section7.note: We adhere to principles of transparency and provide users with the ability to manage cookie usage in accordance with legal requirements.

section8.title: 8. Cookie Policy Updates
section8.content: We may update this Cookie Policy. We will notify you of significant changes via the platform or email.

section9.title: 9. Contact Information
section9.content: For questions related to cookie usage, you can contact us via the feedback form on the platform or by email.

section1.title: 1. Introduction
section1.content: REXTEN Marketplace takes your privacy seriously. This privacy policy describes what data we collect, how we use it, and how we protect it in accordance with US law, including the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and other applicable data protection laws.
section1.content2: REXTEN Marketplace operates the platform only. We are not the seller or performer of services you book; independent businesses and professionals listed on the platform are responsible for those services and for their own legal and tax compliance.

section3.title: 3. How We Use Your Data
section3.content: We use collected data for the following purposes:

list.item1: Providing access to the platform and its features
list.item2: Processing bookings and managing your orders
list.item3: Communicating with you regarding bookings and services
list.item4: Improving platform quality and user experience

subsection1.title: 4.1. Sharing with Service Providers
subsection1.content: When booking a service, we share necessary information with the service provider to fulfill the booking:

subsection2.title: 4.2. Sharing with Service Vendors
subsection2.content: We may share data with third-party services that assist us in operating the platform (hosting, analytics, payment processing). These providers are obligated to protect your data and use it only to provide services to us.

subsection3.title: 4.3. Legal Requirements
subsection3.content: We may disclose your data if required by law or upon request by government authorities.

section5.title: 5. Data Retention
section5.content: We retain your personal data for the period necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this policy, or for the period required by law.
section5.note: Upon account deletion, we delete or anonymize your personal data, unless law requires its retention.

list.item5: Receive a copy of your data in a structured format

subsection2.title: 6.2. California Consumer Rights (CCPA)
subsection2.content: If you are a California resident, you have additional rights under the CCPA:

list.item1: Right to know what personal data is collected and how it is used
list.item2: Right to know if your data is sold to third parties
list.item3: Right to opt-out of the sale of personal data
list.item4: Right to non-discrimination for exercising your rights
list.item5: Right to delete personal data (with some exceptions)

subsection2.note: To exercise your rights, including CCPA rights, contact us via the feedback form on the platform or use the dedicated CCPA request form.

section7.title: 7. Data Security
section7.content: We implement modern technical and organizational measures to protect your data from unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction:

list.item3: Restricted access to data only for authorized employees
list.item4: Regular monitoring and updating of security systems

section8.title: 8. Cookies and Tracking
section8.content: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve platform performance, analyze usage, and personalize content. Detailed information about cookies is available in our Cookie Policy.
section8.note: In accordance with US legal requirements, we provide the ability to manage cookies through browser settings and cookie usage notifications.

section9.title: 9. Children's Privacy (COPPA)
section9.content: Our platform is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 13 without parental consent in accordance with COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act).
section9.note: If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided us with personal data, please contact us to remove such information.

section10.title: 10. Changes to Privacy Policy
section10.content: We may update this Privacy Policy. We will notify you of significant changes via the platform or email.

section11.title: 11. Contact Information
section11.content: For questions related to personal data processing, you can contact us via the feedback form on the platform or by email.
section11.note: For requests related to California consumer rights (CCPA), use the special form on the platform or contact us directly with 'CCPA Request' in the subject line.

section1.title: 1. General Provisions
section1.content: Welcome to REXTEN Marketplace — an online platform operated by REXTEN that connects clients with independent businesses and professionals. REXTEN Marketplace is not a seller, contractor, employer, or direct provider of the services listed; those services are offered by third parties who use the platform. By using our service, you agree to these Terms of Service, governed by US law.
section1.content2: We provide software, discovery, booking tools, communications features, and related technology only. The quality, safety, legality, licensing, insurance, and tax treatment of any service you book are solely between you and the relevant business or professional. These terms apply to all platform users, including residents of all US states, including California.

subsection1.title: 2.1. Client Registration
subsection1.content: To use the platform as a client, you can:

list.item1: Register an account by providing accurate information
list.item2: Use the platform without registration to browse services and book
Not transfer account credentials to third parties
list.item3: Comply with platform rules and legislation
list.item4: Not use the platform for illegal purposes

subsection1.title: 3.1. Service Search
subsection1.content: The platform provides service search capabilities by various criteria: categories, location, rating, price, and other parameters.

subsection2.title: 3.2. Booking
subsection2.content: When you book a service, you enter into a contractual relationship with the business or professional offering that service. REXTEN Marketplace is not a party to that contract. We may display prices and process payments only as a technical and payment facilitator where enabled; the underlying obligation to perform the service remains with the provider.

subsection3.title: 3.3. Booking Cancellation
subsection3.content: Cancellation terms are determined by the specific service provider's policy. We recommend checking cancellation terms before confirming a booking.

section4.title: 4. Review System
section4.content: After receiving a service, you can leave a review about the service quality. Reviews must:

list.item1: Be based on actual service experience
list.item3: Not contain insults, defamation, or false information

section4.note: The platform reserves the right to moderate and remove reviews violating usage rules.

subsection1.title: 5.1. Platform Liability
subsection1.content: REXTEN Marketplace is an intermediary and technology provider. We do not provide the underlying services shown on the platform and are not responsible for the quality, timeliness, legality, or outcome of those services, nor for the acts or omissions of service providers or clients, except where mandatory law requires otherwise.

subsection2.title: 5.2. User Liability
subsection2.content: You are responsible for the accuracy of provided information, compliance with booking terms, and timely payment for services (if applicable).

subsection3.title: 5.3. Service Provider Liability
subsection3.content: The service provider is fully responsible for the quality of services provided, compliance with agreements, and fulfillment of obligations to the client, including permits, taxes, and professional standards applicable to their activity.

section6.title: 6. Intellectual Property
section6.content: All platform materials, including design, text, images, logos, and software, are intellectual property of REXTEN Marketplace or used with permission of rights holders.
section6.note: Use of platform materials without written permission is prohibited, except as provided by law.

section7.title: 7. Modification of Terms
section7.content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Service at any time. We will notify users of significant changes via the platform or email.
section7.note: Continued use of the platform after changes constitutes your acceptance of the new terms.

section8.title: 8. Contact Information
section8.content: For questions related to Terms of Service, you can contact us via the feedback form on the platform or by email.

marketplaceTerms.title: Marketplace Terms
marketplaceTerms.intro: Important: REXTEN Marketplace is a marketplace and software platform. We are not the supplier of the services you book, and we are not your counterparty for the performance of those services.

section1.title: 1. Role of REXTEN Marketplace
section1.content: REXTEN Marketplace enables businesses and professionals to present services and for clients to discover and book them. The platform may also provide tools for scheduling, messaging, and (where available) payment processing.

list.item1: We do not perform the booked services ourselves and do not control how a provider runs their business day to day.
list.item2: Listings, prices, descriptions, and availability are provided by independent providers; they are responsible for their accuracy and lawfulness.
list.item3: Nothing in these documents creates an agency, partnership, employment, or joint venture between REXTEN and any provider or client, unless expressly stated in a separate written agreement.

section2.title: 2. Who you contract with
section2.content: When you purchase or book a service, your agreement for that service is with the listed business or professional (the "Provider"), subject to their terms and applicable law. REXTEN's role is limited to operating the platform and, where applicable, facilitating communication or payment flows as described in the product.

section3.title: 3. Taxes, invoicing, and reporting
section3.content: Providers are independent businesses or self-employed professionals. They are solely responsible for determining and remitting any taxes, social contributions, and fees applicable to their activity in their jurisdiction, and for issuing any required invoices or receipts to clients, unless mandatory law assigns a different obligation.

list.item1: REXTEN does not replace your accountant or tax advisor and does not determine how your transaction should be classified for tax purposes.
list.item2: Fees charged by REXTEN (subscriptions, commissions, or processing) relate to the use of the platform or payment services and are separate from the price of the underlying service.
list.item3: If you need documentation for authorities, request it from the Provider; we may provide transactional records available in your account where the product supports it.

section4.title: 4. Payments
section4.content: Where payments are processed through integrated providers (for example card payments), funds may be routed according to the rules of that payment partner and the Provider's payout settings. REXTEN is not the seller of the underlying service; any platform fee is for marketplace and technology use unless otherwise clearly stated at checkout.

section5.title: 5. Disputes and consumer matters
section5.content: Complaints about service quality, refunds for the service itself, or no-shows should first be directed to the Provider in line with their policy. The platform may offer communication or dispute tools as a convenience but is not obliged to mediate and does not guarantee a particular outcome.

section6.title: 6. Related documents
section6.content: These Marketplace Terms supplement (and do not replace) our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy. If there is a conflict, the document that is more specific to marketplace operations applies to that topic.

meta.title: REXTEN — Service marketplace and business workspace
meta.description: Clients browse and book services; your team runs schedules, CRM, routes, and reports in one connected system.

hero.title: Marketplace for clients, workspace for your team — REXTEN
hero.subtitle: People discover services and book online. You run the business side—schedules, clients, routes, and reporting—in one place, without a patchwork of tools.

features.sectionSubtitle: Public listings bring demand; your dashboard handles day-to-day operations end to end.

brand.title: Listings & online booking

schedule.title: Schedules & routes

marketing.title: Growth & analytics
marketing.description: Promote listings and review performance: bookings, revenue trends, and team activity in dashboards and exports—clear signals for your next step.

industries.sectionSubtitle: The same hybrid setup—client-facing presence plus back-office tools—fits many service models.

cleaning.title: Cleaning Services

beauty.title: Beauty & Wellness

hvac.title: HVAC & Home Services

education.title: Education & Training

testimonials.sectionSubtitle: Short notes from teams that run bookings and operations on REXTEN.

items.name: Sarah Johnson
items.quote: Clients self-book online instead of endless phone tag. The team sees the same schedule and history in one system.

items.name: Michael Chen
items.quote: Multiple shops are easier when workload and calendars stay visible in one workspace—not scattered spreadsheets.

faq.sectionSubtitle: Straight answers about the marketplace and the business workspace. Need more detail? Contact us from your account.

items.question: What is REXTEN?
items.answer: REXTEN combines a client marketplace with a business workspace. Clients search, compare, and book services; you manage schedules, client records, routes where they matter, and reporting in the same account.

items.question: How do I get started?
items.answer: Create an account, add services and team members, and publish availability. Your public listing and booking flow can go live when you are ready.

items.question: How much does it cost?
items.answer: A Free plan is available with usage limits. Paid tiers are Starter, Pro, and Business—the amounts on this page are typical starting prices; confirm current numbers in the product before checkout. The first 14 days of a paid subscription are free.

items.question: Can I import existing clients?
items.answer: Often yes. Send a CSV or spreadsheet export to support and we will walk you through import options that fit your data.

items.question: How do online payments work?
items.answer: Where payments are enabled, checkout uses Stripe. Clients pay by card; payouts follow your Stripe Connect settings.

items.question: Is there a mobile app?
items.answer: Yes—business workflows are available on iOS and Android alongside the web app. Use the links provided in your account for the current build.

items.question: How do I reach support?
items.answer: Use the help or contact options shown in the product. Response times vary by channel and plan; paid tiers may include priority handling where offered.

cta.title: Bring client discovery and your day-to-day work together in one place.
cta.description: List services, take bookings, and manage your team from one workspace. Open your calendar to clients when you are ready.
